message:
fcp-core: drop closure-based push in approval_token_object_id, fix stale success_rate test

Two correctness fixes that surfaced while cross-reviewing recent swarm
work in fcp-core:

* `policy::approval_token_object_id` (committed in 6d273157) used a
  mutable closure to write length-prefixed fields into the fallback
  buffer, then `drop(push)` to release the borrow. clippy fires
  `drop_non_drop` on dropping a non-`Drop` value, which would block
  `cargo clippy -p fcp-core --lib -- -D warnings`. Replace the closure
  with a `[&[u8]; 7]` array of borrowed field slices and a plain `for`
  loop. Behavior and on-the-wire bytes are identical: same domain
  separator, same field order, same length-delimited framing, same
  trailing `signature.is_some()` discriminator.

* `lifecycle::tests::health_metrics_success_rate_two_out_of_three`
  still asserted the truncated `66`, but commit 74d62e6e
  ("Harden numeric accumulators against overflow and precision loss")
  changed `update_health` to `rate.round() as u8`. 2/3 now rounds to
  `67`. Update the assertion and the inline comment to match the new
  (more accurate) behavior; the `update_health` clamp at 100 still
  applies.

Verified locally: `cargo test -p fcp-core --lib` 3911 passed, 1
ignored; `cargo clippy -p fcp-core --lib -- -D warnings` clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
